title: aucuba japonica f. luteomarginata
intro: Aucuba japonica f. luteomarginata, also known as the gold-variegated aucuba, is a plant species within the Aucuba genus, characterized by its gold-edged variegation and deep green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits are believed to include reducing stress, improving immune function, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the gold-variegated aucuba are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety.
